Uh These scores go from 0 to 100.

The other property has been built or renovated asset class.

And then we spit out these scores.

Um So with this, I'll let you know there, there's, in my opinion, four great things about it.
The first being, it's 100% automated.
So there's zero, you know, manual input into this whatsoever.
Zero call arounds.
We're not, you know, inputting data ourselves.
So 100% automated.
The second thing is that we actually update our data every single day.
So as I'm sure you guys are aware, you know, properties out there, they're using these rev tools, they're changing rates regularly.
So our goal is for you guys to be able to jump into here and get that reflection of go of what's exactly going on today.
Um The third thing too, which is nice is that we can actually track any property out there as long as they have a website and use a property management software.
They don't have to be our customers whatsoever.
And for transparency, the way that we collect our data is our tool goes up the property websites, we collect the data from the property websites every single night.
And then for us to get the unit level data, we actually go to the online leasing portion of the property website, which is being powered by that property management software.
So that's kind of why in order for us to track a comp they do have to have a website and use a property management software.
So that's what's allowing you to figure out what a unit was actually leased at versus what was just listed on the website.
Good question.

We do have a section where we provide some transactional data.
Um The way we report that transactional data is one since we're tracking each unit individually, if that unit leaves the property website.
So if unit 123 is there today tomorrow unit 123 is gone.
We report that that unit has been leased.
The price that we associated with the, the lease priced is based off of the last lowest advertised price in the property website.
So there's zero integrations with, with what we're doing here.
We're, we're, we're giving you all this information based off of it.
What's 100% publicly available?
So, since we're not necessarily tied into the system, we don't have access to that private data.
So that's our kind of process for reporting transactional data.

sorry.

Our system can only account for the 25 that are made available.
We have no clue that the other 75 exist.

So the rent the square footage, the price per foot for those 25 that's what's dictating the market.
That's what's influencing the renter.
Um The price for those hidden 75 is being influenced and obviously in what's available on the website.

I'm sorry, it would be occupancy and we call that advertised occupancy for that reason.
